sections.title: Verdict
paragraphs: Anindo is favoured (42.2% win vs 37.2% for Chintu, 20.6% draw). Expected goals: Anindo 2.25 – 1.96 Chintu.
bullets: Anindo has the stronger keeper (0.69 vs 0.33), shifting expected goals against.
Anindo controls the ball better (0.70 vs 0.51).

sections.title: Squad depth (bench impact)
paragraphs: Non-starters with elite per-90 traits add a small squad-depth multiplier to unit ratings (capped at ~5% total; bench cannot dominate outcomes).
Anindo: 3 on bench; 2 with standout quality (+2.4% squad depth boost). Standouts: Pau Cubarsí, Manuel Locatelli. Boosts — attack +0.0%, creation +1.1%, defence +1.3%.
Chintu: 3 on bench; 3 with standout quality (+2.9% squad depth boost). Standouts: Fernandinho, Leandro Trossard, Jordan Pickford. Boosts — attack +0.0%, creation +1.1%, defence +1.8%.

sections.title: Expected goals pipeline
paragraphs: The engine splits attack into finishing and chance-creation channels, then applies opponent suppression and midfield battle modifiers.
Anindo raw attack xG: 2.34 finishing + 0.37 creation = 2.71 before suppression. When Chintu defends, suppression factor is 0.795 (DEF 0.19, mid-def 0.12, GK 0.33, transition risk 0.15).
Chintu raw attack xG: 2.17 + 0.37 = 2.54. Anindo suppression when defending: 0.844 (transition risk 0.24).
Midfield battle multipliers: Anindo ×1.001, Chintu ×0.999. Net xG edge: Anindo by 0.29.

sections.title: Defensive structure & transitions
paragraphs: Anindo xGA suppression: 0.795 (base without transition penalty: 0.782). Chintu: 0.844 (base 0.837).

Anindo fullbacks: Josip Stanišić (RB, exposure 0.30), Dayot Upamecano (LB, exposure 0.18). Team transition risk 0.242 — high attacking fullbacks increase counter vulnerability when midfield cover is thin.
Chintu fullbacks: Djed Spence (RB, exposure 0.16), Malick Thiaw (LB, exposure 0.08). Team transition risk 0.151 — high attacking fullbacks increase counter vulnerability when midfield cover is thin.

sections.title: Formation & squad fit
paragraphs: Anindo (4-3-3): average formation fit 0.75. Chance creation index 0.46, possession 0.70.
Chintu (4-3-3): average formation fit 0.74. Chance creation index 0.47, possession 0.51.

sections.title: Monte Carlo interpretation
paragraphs: Across 500 neutral-venue runs, average scoreline tendency is 2.09–1.92. Both teams score 74.2% of the time; over 2.5 goals 76.6%.
bullets: Trophy boost: Anindo ×1.020, Chintu ×1.006 (applied in simulation units).
